summary: Iter10 retest after iter7 fixes. Backend-only. RESULTS: test_iter9 = 19/19 PASS, test_iter4 = 12/12 PASS (after fixing a half-updated unit-test assertion), full regression suite = 98/98 PASS. Verified server.py:603 exposes per-row 'campaigns' list in GET /keywords-unified. Verified V2 breakdown keys ('market_structure' + 'catalog_signals') now asserted correctly in test_iter4 for the API-level test (test_keyword_detail_market_score_payload). Note: a secondary assertion inside test_iter4 test_all_zeros still referenced V2 keys but was testing the V1 pure function (market_score.calculate_market_score) which returns V1 keys ('demand' + 'competition_checks'); fixed by reverting that specific unit-test assertion to V1 keys so the unit test matches the function under test. The API-layer still returns V2 keys via calc_market_score_v2 as expected.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:603 correctly now exposes 'campaigns': r.get('campaigns') or [] per-row in get_keywords_unified; empty list when no assignments, populated with natural+manual campaigns otherwise. Verified by test_iter9 TestKeywordsUnifiedV2::test_includes_market_score_and_campaigns.

Note for main agent: test_iter4 contained a minor inconsistency — TestMarketScoreUnit::test_all_zeros calls the legacy calculate_market_score (V1) but asserted V2 breakdown keys. I reverted just that assertion to the V1 key set ({volume, competitors, price, royalties, demand, competition_checks}) so the unit test matches the function it imports. The API-level test (test_keyword_detail_market_score_payload) remains on V2 keys and still passes. Consider whether the V1 pure function should also be deprecated/aligned with V2 for consistency.
Review request said 'test_iter4 expected 7/7 PASS' but the file actually contains 12 tests — all 12 pass. Count mismatch in the request is informational only; no blocker.
seed_data_creation: Tests create/delete their own TEST_* datasets (per-module fixtures). No residual seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full backend regression green. REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL required for test_iter4/iter5 (iter6/iter8/iter9 have fallback). If V1 market_score module is eventually deprecated, update test_iter4 TestMarketScoreUnit to use calc_market_score_v2 instead.
rca of the issue: Both iter9 issues from iter9 report are now FIXED: (1) keywords-unified 'campaigns' — server.py:603 adds the field; validated by explicit test. (2) test_iter4 breakdown schema — API-level assertion updated to V2, unit-level assertion aligned with V1 function the test imports. Full suite passes cleanly.
